description:
This image displays two choropleth maps of Europe, including Iceland and the UK, showing Sentinel-2 A&B Level 1C data for 2018 at 1km resolution.

Map (a), titled "Sentinel-2 A&B L1C - Cloudiness", illustrates the percentage of cloud cover. The colour scale ranges from blue (10% cloudiness) to red (100% cloudiness), with intermediate values of 20%, 30%, 40%, 50%, 60%, 70%, 80%, and 90%. High cloudiness (orange to red, 80-100%) is concentrated over the Alps, parts of Central Europe, the UK, and Scandinavia. Lower cloudiness (blue to light blue, 10-50%) is observed across Southern Europe (Spain, Italy, Greece) and Eastern Europe. An inset map focuses on the Alps, showing high cloudiness in mountainous terrain.

Map (b), titled "Sentinel-2 A&B L1C - Average gap", depicts the average temporal gap in observation data, in days. The colour scale ranges from dark blue (3 days) to red (15 days), with intermediate values of 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, and 12 days. The map is overlaid with diagonal stripes, representing Sentinel-2 acquisition orbits. Higher average gaps (orange to red, 10-15 days) are visible in these stripes across Central and Northern Europe, often coinciding with areas of high cloudiness in map (a). Lower average gaps (blue to light blue, 3-7 days) are observed in other stripes, especially prominent in Southern Europe. An inset map shows the Alps area with diagonal stripes indicating varying average gaps.
